creator: matthias.pfaller@familie-pfaller.de
text:
I tried to open the library I had created with an earlier version of calibre. With deskutils/calibre this fails.
When opening the same library (via samba) on a windows machine using calibre 5.6 it will just work without any complains. There was only one instance running at any time.

When I remove the calibre configuration and do a fresh start, it will not create an empty library:
calibre, version 5.6.0
Corrupted database: The library database at /home/leo/Calibre Library appears to be corrupted. Do you want calibre to try and rebuild it automatically? The rebuild may not be completely successful. If you say No, a new empty calibre library will be created.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/gui2/main.py", line 315, in initialize_db
    db = LibraryDatabase(self.library_path)
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/legacy.py", line 191, in __init__
    load_user_formatter_functions=not is_second_db)
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/legacy.py", line 48, in create_backend
    load_user_formatter_functions=load_user_formatter_functions)
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/backend.py", line 453, in __init__
    SchemaUpgrade(self, self.library_path, self.field_metadata)
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/schema_upgrades.py", line 32, in __init__
    meth()
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/schema_upgrades.py", line 53, in upgrade_version_1
    ''')
  File "/usr/local/lib/calibre/calibre/db/backend.py", line 920, in execute
    return self.conn.cursor().execute(sql, bindings)
  File "src/statementcache.c", line 386, in sqlite3_prepare
apsw.SQLError: SQLError: no such table: main.books
